summary: Sprint 9 — Real Integrations Hub (6 providers scaffolded) + Password Lifecycle (change/admin-reset/forgot/reset). Backend: 18/18 sprint pytest cases green (test_real_integrations.py 9/9, test_password_lifecycle.py 9/9). Sprint regression: 38/38 green across test_pdf_docs.py, test_register_and_crm.py, test_catalog_and_access.py, test_sprint_extras.py, test_crm_enrichment.py. Combined 56/56 sprint+regression green. Frontend E2E validated: /portal/forgot-password, /portal/reset-password (with & without ?token), /portal/{admin|client}/settings/password (change-password happy + wrong-current-pw error + same-password-must-differ error), /portal/admin/real-integrations (all 6 provider cards render, Midtrans card expands to expose Test-connection + Save buttons), /portal/admin/users Reset-pw modal (47 buttons, modal opens with admin-reset-new/confirm/notify/submit testids), sidebar change-pw-link (lock icon) present for both admin and client. Regression sanity: demo@client.com login → dashboard OK, order wizard step1 loads, invoices list loads. Backend correctly returns 403 to client on admin integration endpoints.

issues: No client-side role guard on the route — a client role that navigates directly to /portal/admin/real-integrations sees the React dev-overlay ('Uncaught runtime errors: Request failed with status code 403') instead of being redirected to /portal/client/dashboard or shown a friendly '403 Not authorized' page. Backend is correct (returns 403). This overlay only appears in dev build but still indicates a missing try/catch or route guard on the AdminRealIntegrations page's initial data fetch. Not a security defect — a UX defect.

backend_pytest_sprint.test_real_integrations.py: 9/9 passed — schema returns 6 providers, client 403 on schema, list+upsert+masking (server_key redacted with SB-M**** helper, empty credentials merge does NOT wipe secret), test endpoint on unconfigured, unknown provider 404, proxmox/mikrotik/pay-online live endpoints all return 400 when disabled, webhook rejects bogus signature (400 or 404)
backend_pytest_sprint.test_password_lifecycle.py: 9/9 passed — wrong current pw 400, same pw 400, weak (<8) 422, happy-path change (old fails, new works), admin reset (old fails, new works, notify_user honoured), client cannot reset others (403), forgot-password neutral (same message for known & unknown emails), forgot->log-token-extract->reset one-shot (2nd use returns 400), bad-token 400

backend_regression.test_pdf_docs.py + test_register_and_crm: 38/38 passed in 16.72s — invoice PDF still returns application/pdf, self-registration still mirrors to CRM, order wizard pricing math unchanged, admin CRM enrichment KPIs still render

frontend_public_password_screens.login_forgot_link: data-testid=login-goto-forgot present on /portal/login and navigates to /portal/forgot-password
frontend_public_password_screens.forgot_page: Single email input + [data-testid=forgot-submit] Send-reset-link button. After submit, form is replaced with [data-testid=forgot-done] neutral success card
frontend_public_password_screens.reset_page_no_token: Renders error banner ('token missing/invalid/expired') when ?token= query param is absent
frontend_public_password_screens.reset_page_with_token: ?token=abc123 renders full form with all 4 testids: reset-form, reset-new, reset-confirm, reset-submit

frontend_change_password.sidebar_lock_icon_admin: [data-testid=change-pw-link] visible in admin sidebar user card, navigates to /portal/admin/settings/password
frontend_change_password.sidebar_lock_icon_client: [data-testid=change-pw-link] visible in client sidebar user card
frontend_change_password.form_fields: change-pw-current + change-pw-new + change-pw-confirm + change-pw-submit all render
frontend_change_password.wrong_current_pw: Backend returns 400 → inline red banner appears in UI
frontend_change_password.same_password: Inline red banner 'New password must differ from the current one.' displays correctly

frontend_admin_users_reset_modal.reset_buttons: 47 [data-testid=reset-pw-*] buttons in the Users table (one per row)
frontend_admin_users_reset_modal.modal_testids: admin-reset-new + admin-reset-confirm + admin-reset-notify (checkbox default checked) + admin-reset-submit all render inside the modal
frontend_admin_users_reset_modal.modal_copy: 'Set a new password for <Name> (<email>).' correctly personalised

frontend_real_integrations.provider_cards: All 6 cards render: Proxmox VE (proxmox, DISABLED), Mikrotik RouterOS (mikrotik, DISABLED), Midtrans Snap (midtrans, DISABLED), Xendit (xendit, ENABLED), Duitku (duitku, ENABLED), SMTP Outgoing Email (smtp, DISABLED)
frontend_real_integrations.expansion: Clicking Midtrans header reveals credentials + options section and both 'Test connection' and 'Save' CTAs
frontend_real_integrations.client_role: Backend returns 403; frontend UI does NOT gate the route with a role guard so a client that visits the URL sees the React dev-overlay for the uncaught AxiosError (see design_issues)

regression_sanity.admin_dashboard_KPIs: Total clients 36, Active services 7, Pending orders 4, Open tickets 9, Revenue (month) Rp 3,052,500 — all still render

action_items: OPTIONAL / LOW PRIORITY — Add a client-side role guard (or try/catch + friendly banner) on /portal/admin/real-integrations so a non-admin who navigates there is redirected to /portal/client/dashboard rather than seeing the React dev-overlay for the 403 AxiosError. Same pattern likely already applied on other admin-only routes — copy that guard. Backend is correct.
critical_code_review_comments: portal/integrations_v2.py — redact() correctly masks sensitive fields (secret/password/api_key/token) with *_masked helper (e.g., server_key_masked='SB-M****'). PUT merge is correctly non-destructive when a credential value is empty — the test_list_and_upsert_and_masking test proves the server_key survives a subsequent PUT with only client_key set.
portal/routes.py password-reset — hashed at rest (sha256), single-use (marked used=true), 60-min expiry — all three enforced. Old-password login returns 401 after any change/reset — good.

Forgot-password no-enumeration is real: both known & unknown emails return the identical neutral message body, so an attacker cannot enumerate users. SMTP fallback logs 'token=<url-safe>' to backend.err.log — this is a scaffold-only behaviour and clearly documented in the review context.
Webhook /webhooks/midtrans returns 400 or 404 on bogus signatures (never 200) — signature verification is enforced before any state mutation. Good.

AdminRealIntegrations.jsx — missing client-side role guard on the initial data fetch. When a client role lands on the route, the axios 403 propagates as an unhandled promise rejection and triggers the dev-overlay. Not a security bug (backend blocks) but a UX polish miss.
test_real_integrations.py test method name is 'test_schema_returns_5_providers' but actually asserts 6 providers — cosmetic rename recommended for future maintainers.

test_real_integrations.py::test_test_endpoint_on_unconfigured_returns_ok_message is essentially a no-op (only writes settings, no assertions). Recommend adding an explicit assert on the /midtrans/test response when misconfigured to raise the value of the test.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all sprint-9 UI features render + integrate correctly; one minor UX polish miss on client-role hitting admin URL directly)

seed_data_creation: Sprint tests auto-create disposable pwtest_*/admreset_*/forgot_* users (each cleaned up in fixture teardown). Midtrans integration record is left in a 'saved sandbox credentials' state after the masking test — this is expected (test verifies persistence).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 9 is fully green. Key artefacts: (1) Backend — 9/9 test_real_integrations.py + 9/9 test_password_lifecycle.py + 38/38 sprint-regression. (2) All 6 integration providers appear on /portal/admin/real-integrations (proxmox/mikrotik/midtrans/xendit/duitku/smtp). Each has Enabled toggle + credentials block (with saved:*** placeholders) + options + Test-connection + Save. (3) Password screens: /portal/forgot-password (public), /portal/reset-password?token=... (public), /portal/{admin|client}/settings/password (auth). Admin sidebar + client sidebar both show a lock-icon quick-link. (4) AdminUsers has a Reset-pw button on every row → modal → confirms with browser confirm() → POST /admin/users/{uid}/reset-password. (5) FORGOT-PASSWORD LOG-TOKEN EXTRACTION works via /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log 'token=<url-safe>' regex — if the pod is re-provisioned the log rolls and the test will skip (documented in review request). (6) One minor UX polish miss: no client-side role guard on /portal/admin/real-integrations — non-admins that navigate there see the React dev-overlay for a 403 AxiosError instead of a friendly redirect. Backend is correct. Recommend main agent add a role check similar to the other admin routes. Do NOT re-attempt the 4 pre-existing test_portal.py data-drift failures — they are documented as expected.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs to root-cause. The one polish miss (unhandled 403 on client-visits-admin-URL) is documented in action_items.
